title: Error emulating x86_64 machine on arm64 Mac
body:
### Issue Description

I'm having issues running an x86_64 Podman machine on my M1 Mac. I created the new machine by using the default procedure with an additional "--image" argument to specify an x86_64 OS. When I try to start the machine, I get the following error:

```
% podman machine start podman-machine-x86_64
Starting machine "podman-machine-x86_64"
Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 0
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Create a new machine:
```
podman machine init --image ~/Code/Images/fedora-coreos-39.20240225.3.0-metal.x86_64.raw podman-machine-x86_64
```

> Image I used: https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/prod/streams/stable/builds/39.20240225.3.0/x86_64/fedora-coreos-39.20240225.3.0-metal.x86_64.raw.xz

2. Start the new machine:
```
podman machine start podman-machine-x86_64
```


### Describe the results you received

I get the following output:

```
Starting machine "podman-machine-x86_64"
Error: vfkit exited unexpectedly with exit code 0
```

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the machine to start and emulate an x86_64 machine on my arm-based Mac.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: You should run the ARM machine on the M1 not the x86.  If you want to build x86 apps you can do this in the ARM machine with qemu-user-static.
Thanks for your reply @rhatdan. I'm not familiar with qemu-user-static. Does it still work on applehv based machines?
UPDATE: I'm able to build and run x86 images on the default arm64 Podman machine. So maybe that's already good enough for me.
